commands.purpose: List active + optionally expired sessions. Filterable by user_id, identity_id, application_id. Redacted — PAT plaintext + hashes never shown. Useful for support-driven session investigation without touching HTTP admin surfaces.

commands.signature: auth:revoke-session {id : PAT id (integer or ULID form)} {--reason=}
commands.purpose: Revoke a specific session by PAT id. Fires SessionRevoked(reason=admin_revocation). --reason is written to the audit log; required in production per auth.commands.require_reason.
usage_examples: php artisan auth:revoke-session 12345 --reason='Support ticket #67890, user reported unfamiliar session'

commands.purpose: Bulk revoke every active PAT + refresh token for a User or Identity (cross-Application). Dispatches RevokeAllActiveSessionsJob. --confirm required in production to prevent accidental bulk revocation.
usage_examples: php artisan auth:revoke-all-sessions --user=usr_01H... --reason='User requested account lockdown' --confirm
php artisan auth:revoke-all-sessions --identity=idn_01H... --reason='Suspected credential compromise — cross-Application lockdown' --confirm

commands.purpose: Rotate to a new JWT signing key. --application binds to a single Application; omit to rotate the global platform-plane key. Old key marked retired_at=now(); still verify-capable through auth.jwt.rotation_grace_period_days. --force skips the 25-days-since-last-rotation guard (used for incident response).
usage_examples: php artisan auth:rotate-jwt-key --reason='Global key rotation — KMS incident response' --force

commands.purpose: List JWT signing keys. Redacted — secret_encrypted NEVER shown. Includes kid, algorithm, application binding, active state, timestamps.
Add a jti to the JWT deny list. Fires JwtRevoked. --natural-expiry MUST match the original JWT's exp so the deny-list row auto-purges when no longer needed.

Run every purge job synchronously: refresh tokens, password resets, email verifications, MFA challenges, cross-app grants, JWT deny list. --dry-run reports counts without deleting. Useful for offline environments where the scheduler isn't running.
Sign a synthetic JWT with the specified kid + verify the signature round-trips. Never emits the shared secret. Used to validate a rotation worked before switching production traffic.

commands.signature: auth:describe-token {token : Token identifier — Sanctum PAT id, refresh ULID, jti, or reset/verification token ULID}
commands.purpose: Print token metadata (issued_at, expires_at, consumed_at, revoked_at, kind, tokenable). Redacted — the token secret / hash / signature is NEVER emitted. Used for debugging session issues.
